description: Gets information about the current working location or directory.
Creates a new directory and any missing parent directories.

flags.meaning: Specifies that the new item is a folder.
Creates parent directories if they don't exist without throwing an error.

description: Deletes the specified items, including all child items, forcefully.

flags.meaning: Deletes the items in the specified locations and in all child items.
Allows the cmdlet to remove items that cannot otherwise be changed, such as hidden or read-only files.

description: Searches for a specific file recursively, suppressing permission denied errors.

flags.meaning: Specifies a filter to qualify the Path parameter.
Gets the items in the specified locations and in all child items.
Hides errors, useful for directories you don't have permission to read.

description: Gets the content of the item at the specified location.
Lists the top 15 running processes sorted by highest CPU usage.

flags.meaning: Sorts the output in descending order.
Selects only the first 15 objects from the pipeline.

description: Gets a list of all processes running on the local computer.
Stops one or more running processes using their Process ID.

flags.meaning: Specifies the process IDs of the processes to stop.
Stops the process immediately, bypassing any confirmation prompts.

description: Stops all processes with the specified name.

flags.meaning: Specifies the process names to be stopped (without the .exe extension).
Forces the process to stop without prompting for user confirmation.

description: Starts a local process and hides its window from the user.

flags.meaning: Specifies the path and name of the program to run.
Starts the process with a hidden window so it runs entirely in the background.

description: Creates a new local user account on the system without an initial password.

flags.meaning: Specifies the name of the new user account.
Creates the account without requiring a secure string password upfront.

description: Securely prompts for a new password and assigns it to the target user.

flags.meaning: Masks the password input in the console for security.
Applies the secure string to the target account.

description: Launches a new PowerShell session using the credentials of another user.

flags.meaning: Prompts for the password of the specified user account to launch the process.

description: Retrieves a formatted list of all local user accounts, including their enabled status.
Deletes a local user account. Note: This does not automatically delete their profile data folder.

flags.meaning: Specifies the exact name of the local user account to remove.

description: Gets a consolidated object of system and operating system properties.
Gets the specified Volume object, displaying health status, file system type, size, and remaining free space.
Gets the time elapsed since the last boot of the operating system (requires PowerShell 6+).
Restarts the operating system on local and remote computers.

flags.meaning: Forces the computer to restart immediately, even if other users are logged on or programs are running.

description: Starts one or more stopped services.

flags.meaning: Specifies the exact service names for the service to be started.

description: Changes the user preference for the PowerShell execution policy, determining which scripts are allowed to run.

flags.meaning: Requires that all scripts and configuration files downloaded from the internet are signed by a trusted publisher.
Applies the execution policy only to the current logged-in user, avoiding system-wide registry changes.

description: Takes ownership of a file or directory and gives ownership to the local Administrators group instead of the current user.

flags.meaning: Specifies the target file or directory.
Gives ownership to the Administrators group instead of the currently logged-on user.

description: Launches a new PowerShell window with elevated Administrator privileges (triggers UAC prompt).

flags.meaning: Instructs Windows to trigger the User Account Control (UAC) prompt to elevate the process.

description: Retrieves the underlying .NET identity object to accurately output the domain and username of the current session.
Generates a new SSH key pair natively in PowerShell via the built-in OpenSSH client.

flags.meaning: Specifies the modern, highly secure Ed25519 cryptographic algorithm.
Adds a descriptive comment to the public key file.

description: Sends ICMP echo request packets, or pings, to one or more computers. Returns robust PowerShell objects instead of pure text.

flags.meaning: Specifies the computers to ping.
Specifies the number of echo requests to send.

description: Gets the IP network configuration, including interfaces, IP addresses, and DNS servers.
Displays diagnostic information for a connection, including a full trace route to the target host.

flags.meaning: Indicates that the cmdlet performs a route trace to the destination.

description: Gets current TCP connections, showing local/remote ports and IP addresses as filterable objects.
Performs a DNS query for the specified name and returns a structured object containing the records.

flags.meaning: Specifies the domain name to resolve.

description: Displays the state of the working directory and the staging area in a Git repository.
Sends an HTTP or HTTPS request to a RESTful web service and automatically parses the JSON/XML response into objects.

flags.meaning: Specifies the Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) of the Internet resource to which the web request is sent.
Specifies the method used for the web request (e.g., Get, Post, Put, Delete).

description: Finds text in strings and files, functioning similarly to grep on UNIX systems.

flags.meaning: Specifies the path to the files to search.
Specifies the text or regular expression to find.
Searches in the specified directory and all its subdirectories.

description: Extracts files from a specified zipped archive file to a specified destination folder.

flags.meaning: Specifies the path to the archive file (does not interpret wildcard characters).
Specifies the path to the folder where you want the archive extracted.

description: Retrieves the environment variables configured for the current PowerShell session by accessing the Env: drive.
Clears the display in the host program, bringing the prompt to the top of the window.
Gets a list of the commands entered during the current PowerShell session.
Gets content from a web page on the Internet and saves it directly to a local file.

flags.meaning: Specifies the URL of the file you want to download.
Specifies the local path and file name to save the download as.

description: Displays information about PowerShell cmdlets and concepts, including detailed examples.

flags.meaning: Adds parameter descriptions and concrete examples to the basic help display.

description: Uses the native Windows tree utility inside PowerShell to display directory structures.

flags.meaning: Displays the names of the files in each folder.

description: Retrieves the exact process object running on a specific local port using native PowerShell networking cmdlets.

flags.meaning: Specifies the exact Process ID (PID) to retrieve.
Filters the TCP connections to only show those using local port 80.

description: Gets a robust object representing the current date and time.
Creates or changes an alias (alternate name) for a cmdlet or command in the current session.

flags.meaning: Specifies the new alias name.
Specifies the name of the cmdlet or command being aliased.

description: Displays a highly readable, UNIX-style formatted string representing the current day, date, and year.

flags.meaning: Displays the date and time in UNIX format.

description: Reads the content of a file and pipes it into a measurement object to count lines, words, and characters.

flags.meaning: Counts the total number of lines.

description: Uses a native PowerShell while loop to continuously clear the screen and execute a command every 2 seconds.

flags.meaning: Specifies how long the script should pause (in seconds) before looping again.

description: Generates a highly secure, 16-character random password using all printable ASCII characters (letters, numbers, and symbols).

flags.meaning: Specifies the length of the generated password string.

description: Deletes all the contents of the DNS client cache using a native PowerShell cmdlet.
Leverages the underlying .NET framework within PowerShell to accurately encode a UTF-8 string into Base64.
Retrieves the default IP route and isolates the gateway IP address (NextHop) and the network adapter name.

flags.meaning: Filters the routing table for the universal default route.

description: Retrieves the basic properties of all network adapters, including their physical status, MAC address, and link speed.
